response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong web, TypeScript, and D-language backgrounds, but he does not meet the core requirements for this embedded security role. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, no security-protocol work (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), and only brief, dated C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows, 2014-2015) rather than sustained C-for-UNIX development. His low-latency DLang systems work shows systems aptitude, but the domain gap in embedded networking and switch firmware is too large.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical teaching/workshops
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed; the role requires native-level Japanese
No demonstrated Linux kernel, network-switch, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF) experience
C experience is minimal and dated; recent career is web/full-stack TypeScript, not embedded firmware
